meta.title: The Epistemic Immune System: Architecting Zero-Trust Semantic Validation in Agent Swarms
meta.short_tagline: A stratified, three-tier cryptographic and semantic validation architecture for multi-agent LLM swarms. It prevents latent space poisoning by cryptographically binding an agent's causal reasoning lineage to its decentralized identity.

overview.title: System Overview
overview.intro: Standard transport-layer security (TLS, API keys) is fundamentally blind to the semantic integrity of agent payloads. A legitimately authenticated agent that has been compromised can legally broadcast corrupted reasoning to the swarm. The Epistemic Immune System solves this verification gap by providing a stratified, multi-tier defense mechanism that bridges deterministic cryptography and probabilistic latent spaces, structurally preventing Logic-layer Prompt Control Injections (LPCI) from contaminating the swarm's shared memory.

summary_card.label: Core Philosophy
summary_card.title: Semantic Integrity Surpasses Transport Identity
summary_card.body: Validating an agent via static tokens confirms its origin but ignores its cognitive integrity. True zero-trust requires mathematically verifying the causal lineage and semantic intent of the payload itself before granting it entry to shared vector stores.

key_concepts.title: Core Paradigms

cards.title: Logic-layer Prompt Control Injection (LPCI)
cards.body: The primary semantic threat vector where dormant, conditionally-activated malicious commands are embedded in an agent's persistent episodic memory. These survive session boundaries and bypass conventional perimeter defenses.

cards.title: Stratified Proof System
cards.body: A bi-modal validation architecture that mirrors biological immunity. It combines a fast, low-cost semantic fingerprinting layer (innate immunity) using Locality-Sensitive Hashing (LSH) with a suspicion-activated zkSNARK reasoning lineage verifier (adaptive immunity) to manage computational latency.

cards.title: Sovereign Identity Graphs (SIG)
cards.body: An identity layer utilizing W3C Decentralized Identifiers (DIDs) and Verifiable Credentials (VCs), mapped to a continuous Bayesian decay function. An agent's trust score dynamically degrades over time unless continuously refreshed by verifiable, high-fidelity cognitive outputs.

key_concepts.intro: Foundational pillars bridging probabilistic latent space semantics and deterministic cryptographic verification.

workflow.title: Validation Pipeline

steps.label: Tier 3: Identity & Trust Verification
steps.summary: Check agent identity and continuous trust scores before inspecting semantic content.
bullets: Resolve the agent's W3C DID document.
Verify the Verifiable Credentials (model hash, scope, alignment certifications).
Assess the Bayesian trust score; automatically quarantine if it falls below the minimum threshold (0.25).

steps.label: Tier 1: Semantic Fingerprinting (Innate Immunity)
steps.summary: Perform high-speed LSH evaluation to detect potentially harmful intent drift.
bullets: Pass the inbound payload through a SOTA semantic embedding model to generate a dense vector.
Compute a 512-bit SimHash fingerprint from the vector.
Calculate normalized Hamming distance against the agent's historical baseline.
Flag for Tier 2 escalation if drift exceeds 0.28 threshold or rolling z-score > 3.0.

steps.label: Tier 2: Cryptographic Lineage (Adaptive Immunity)
steps.summary: Execute deep cryptographic proofs on flagged or randomly audited payloads.
bullets: Recompute the Merkle root from the agent's intermediate Chain-of-Thought states.
Verify zkSNARK proofs of inference without exposing private model weights.
Penalize trust score and isolate to Quarantine Escrow upon failure, or merge to Chrono-RAG vector space on success.

workflow.intro: The operational sequence applied to all inter-agent syndication payloads via the Epistemic Validation Node.

checklist.title: Implementation Checklist

items.label: Identity Graph Foundation
bullets: Deploy a distributed ledger for W3C DID registration.
Implement continuous trust score decay module using TimescaleDB.
Establish deterministic external binaries for VC verification.

items.label: Tier 1 Semantic Firewall Setup
bullets: Standardize on a specific, frozen embedding model hash for the entire swarm.
Implement a 512-plane SimHash logic in a high-performance environment (Rust/Go).
Deploy dynamic baseline updating mechanisms (EMA alpha=0.05) to accommodate legitimate agent learning.

items.label: Tier 2 Cryptography & Escrow Configuration
bullets: Integrate verifiable inference compilers (e.g., ZKTorch) for zero-knowledge proofs.
Set up a strictly segregated Quarantine Escrow buffer (Redis) with 7-day retention.
Enforce JWS signature requirements on all agent vector updates.

checklist.intro: Actionable technical steps required to deploy the zero-trust epistemic layer.

metrics.intro: Key performance indicators for evaluating the efficacy and latency of the immune system.

items.name: Cosine Distance Delta
items.description: The drift measured between the incoming payload embedding and the agent's historical SIG baseline.

items.name: ZKP Verification Latency
items.description: The time required in milliseconds to verify the zkSNARK proof of reasoning lineage per payload.

items.name: Trust Score Decay Function
items.description: Continuous Bayesian evaluation tracking the agent lifecycle to prevent Chronological Saponification.

metrics.title: Success Metrics

personas.intro: Key structural roles in maintaining and operating the multi-agent epistemic layer.

cells: Define constitutional boundaries and tier threshold parameters
Submit cryptographically verifiable inferences and reasoning lineages
Issue Verifiable Credentials and process Bayesian trust updates

personas.title: Ecosystem Personas

failure_modes.name: Semantic Hash Collision
failure_modes.description: An adversary mathematically crafts a prompt injection that entirely shifts semantic payload intent while perfectly replicating the original agent's LSH bin assignment and Merkle Root.
mitigations: Maintain dynamic random sampling (5%) for Tier 2 zkSNARK audits even on clean Tier 1 passes.
Use proprietary, randomly-seeded hyperplanes for SimHash projections.

failure_modes.name: Thermodynamic Latency Tax Cascades
failure_modes.description: Cryptographic validation over-engineering leads to cascading latency failures, stalling the entire multi-agent orchestrator.
mitigations: Lightweight TensorCommitments implementation for fallback non-ZKP audits.

failure_modes.name: Legitimate Epistemic Evolution Masking
failure_modes.description: A sophisticated attacker times injections to coincide with genuine swarm learning events, exploiting the legitimate drift exemption window.
mitigations: Rate-sensitive anomaly detection measuring rolling z-scores over 64-payload windows to detect velocity rather than strictly magnitude.

resilience.intro: Identified vulnerabilities, structural limitations, and deployed mathematical mitigations.
resilience.title: System Resilience & Failure Modes

structure.intro: The C4 mapping components constituting the Epistemic validation node pipeline.

cells: Executes Innate Semantic Immunity (Tier 1) via LSH fingerprinting
Executes Adaptive Cryptographic Immunity (Tier 2) via lineage proofs
Manages Identity layer (Tier 3) and Autophagic Trust Ledger
Isolates anomalous payloads for forensic evaluation
